mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% as of 2016-10-15 indicates sustained low-rate regime
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144,952 thousand as of 2016-10-15 indicates near-threshold employment level
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with full employment approaching
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite strong payroll growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rise in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals accelerating consumer demand and construction activity, which interact to boost corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 465,914 million and HOUST at 1,047 thousand as of 2016-09-15 indicate concurrent demand strength
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to outperform either univariate signal alone by > 10% in Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below the 104-index level signals weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side constraints that compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate earnings downgrades.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.226 as of 2016-09-15 is near trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite INDPRO weakness, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rise of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as recession risk premia increase.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% as of 2016-10-15 is near historical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle peak with labor market tightening reversal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment rise, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration above 2.5% annualized signals strengthening economic momentum that lifts corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors increase equity allocations in response to improved growth outlook.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,702.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2016-07-15
regime_hint: post-election fiscal stimulus expectations with growth acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite GDP acceleration, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month > 0.3%) creates a negative real rate environment that incentivizes equity investment as a real asset hedge, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% and CPIAUCSL at 241.002 as of 2016-09-15
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to outperform either univariate signal by > 10% in Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with rising payroll employment signals synchronized supply and labor expansion that sustains profit margins and capital investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors increase cyclical equity exposure.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.226 and PAYEMS at 144,952 thousand as of 2016-09-15 indicate concurrent supply-labor strength
regime_hint: synchronized expansion with full capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to outperform either univariate signal by > 10% in Sharpe ratio.
